pr.title: Improve matching across multiple source files
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Large matching-improvement PR spanning 95 files and many Melee subsystems. It reported 36 new matches, 117 unmatched-item improvements, +26,828 matched-code bytes, +1,064 matched-data bytes, and no broken matches or metric regressions. The work mixed local expression/control-flow reshaping, type/view structs for offset-sensitive access, stack/local ordering changes, and review-driven cleanup to preserve readability and project conventions.
what_changed: Improved matching across 85 changed units and 95 files, with 34 newly matched functions and 2 newly matched sections according to the PR report.
Raised fuzzy match from 97.428474 to 97.461624 and matched code from 70.79643% to 71.48751%, with no broken matches, no fuzzy regressions, and no metric regressions.

Added offset-view structs in THPDec.c such as THPMCURowFields, THPFileInfoMCUBufferView, and THPFileInfoDCTComp*View to access fields at known offsets without claiming the full THPFileInfo layout was understood.

Reworked THP MCU row decompression code to use an info-derived MCU buffer view, explicit restart-field casts, and DCT component predDC views; this improved __THPDecompressiMCURow640x480 and related THP decode routines.
Adjusted camera.c local stack layout, condition shape, direct cam->transform field writes, and hoisted temporaries in render-priority code to improve matching while keeping the logic recognizable.
Improved lbaudio_ax.c substantially, including new full matches for fn_800253D8 and fn_800256BC per the largest-new-matches report.
Converted reviewed ftCo_09F7 dispatch code from goto-like control flow into a grouped switch after review suggested the structure was likely one or more switch statements.
Removed review-rejected matching aids including register keywords in ftCo_Bury.c and gm_19EF.c, a new optimizer pragma in gm_1832.c, and new M2C_FIELD usage in grinishie1.c.

Restored inline float and string constants in ftcoll.c, gm_1832.c, gm_1A4C.c, gmregclear.c, and ifprize.c after review feedback that such constants should remain readable and will be resolved during linking.
Kept decomp-orchestrator artifacts out of the actual Melee PR while using orchestrator regression checks to verify no regressions.
smart_moves: Used project-wide regression reporting as the acceptance gate: ninja, ninja changes_all, main.dol OK, GitHub CI, and an orchestrator regression check all passed with zero regressions.
Separated generated/local orchestration artifacts from source changes, explicitly keeping decomp-orchestrator/ and local artifacts out of the Melee PR.
Where full struct knowledge was weak, introduced narrow view structs with padding and only the needed field, avoiding overclaiming global header layouts while still producing better codegen.
Accepted reviewer readability constraints even where less readable changes might have helped matching, preserving maintainability by restoring inline literals and avoiding banned keywords/pragmas.
Replaced a goto dispatch in ftCo_09F7 with a grouped switch after review, showing that matching can often be improved without making control flow worse.
Used direct containing-object field access in camera.c, such as cam->transform.target_fov, when it better expressed the generated code than only using an alias pointer.
decomp_lessons: Do not introduce register, asm, or similar keywords as matching hacks in normal C decomp code; reviewers explicitly asked to remove register in ftCo_Bury.c and gm_19EF.c.
Prefer inline string and float literals over manually naming every constant symbol just for matching/readability reasons; reviewers noted these are resolved during linking.
Avoid adding optimizer pragmas as a local matching crutch unless there is strong project acceptance; the new pragma in gm_1832.c was requested to be removed.
When m2c-style goto dispatch appears to encode a dense branch table, try grouped switch statements before accepting goto-heavy code.
Offset-view structs can be useful for matching when a large SDK/game struct layout is uncertain, but they should be kept narrow and evidence-based.
Before moving string tables into named char arrays, consider whether it makes the source less readable and whether linker behavior already handles the desired placement.
A broad matching PR should report both new matches and regressions; the strongest evidence here was the explicit zero broken matches and zero metric/fuzzy regressions.
Review cleanup can be as important as raw matching improvements: the final merged version incorporated convention fixes while preserving regression-free metrics.
naming_conventions: Temporary or unknown functions retained existing address-based names such as fn_800253D8, fn_800256BC, fn_8018325C, fn_80165AC0, and grOldYoshi_8020EC10.
THP offset-view helper structs were named by subsystem and purpose, e.g. THPMCURowFields, THPFileInfoMCUBufferView, THPFileInfoDCTCompYView, THPFileInfoDCTCompUView, and THPFileInfoDCTCompVView.
Existing Melee file/module prefixes were preserved, including ftCo_, gm_, gr, lb, it, and Camera_.
Review feedback favored readable inline literals over creating many address-like names such as gm_803DB640 for strings.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Stack/local ordering and pad-size adjustments were used, visible in camera.c changing a local padding array from 16 to 8 bytes and reordering float temporaries.
Boolean condition shape was changed in camera.c by folding multi-step checks into a negated compound condition using comma expressions and abs_threshold_inline.
Alias selection mattered: some camera.c code changed from transform->field through a pointer alias to cam->transform.field or cam->transform_copy.field.

THPDec.c used pointer casts to offset-view structs to force field access at specific offsets, including MCUsPerRow at 0x8CC, MCU buffers at offset 0x10, and predDC fields at component-specific offsets.
THPDec.c changed huffman table num_Vij storage to an explicit u16 write through pad2 and narrowed a result local from int to u8, likely for type/codegen matching.
THPDec.c replaced global __THPMCUBuffer indexing in some row-decode paths with an info-relative mcuBuffer pointer/view.
Control-flow restructuring included replacing goto-style dispatch with a grouped switch in ftCo_09F7 after review.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: The structure looked like one or more big switch statements; reviewer asked to try matching it as a switch. Author later reported converting the reviewed goto dispatch into a grouped switch while preserving matching behavior.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Do not use register, asm, or similar keywords. Author later reported removing register usage.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Remove register. Author later reported removing register usage.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Keep constants like strings and floats inlined; they will be resolved during linking.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Same inline-literal feedback for string constants, and separately requested removal of a new optimizer pragma. Author later reported restoring literals and removing the pragma.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Revert changes that split many THP filename literals into named char arrays because they were less readable and would be resolved during linking.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Ditto feedback on keeping literals inline rather than extracting constants.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Author reported removing newly added M2C_FIELD usage in response to review cleanup.

searchable_terms: Improve matching across multiple source files
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching THPFileInfo layout or replacing THPDec offset-view structs with real headers.
Search for remaining register or asm keyword usage introduced for matching in Melee source files.
Search for remaining extracted string or float constants that reviewers may prefer to inline.
Review ftCo_09F7 grouped switch in later history to see if more dispatch tables were recovered.
Inspect particle.c context-slot type fix mentioned in verification for a concrete struct/header lesson.
Check whether gm_1A4C THP filename arrays remained inline and whether linker placement stayed stable.
